title: Pleiostachya morlae
intro: Pleiostachya morlae, commonly known as Morinda root, is a plant species native to Madagascar and is a type of rubiaceous plant. The health benefits of Morinda root include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and promote overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Morinda root include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthraquinones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Morinda root is often used to make tea, capsules, and tinctures, which are valued for their potential to improve cardiovascular health and boost the immune system.

Its roots are used to make a tea that is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is also thought to possess antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating infections. In traditional medicine, Pleiostachya morlae is often used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. However, more research is needed to confirm its therapeutic effects and potential side effects.
constituents: Pleiostachya morlae is a plant species that is widely used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia.
